headline: What's New In the Consumer Tech World Last Week? News That You Should Know (June 30 - July 5, 2024)
summary: The Biden administration has allocated $504 million to boost regional technology hubs across the US. CA is voting on an AI safety bill opposed by tech giants. Epic Games plans to relaunch Fortnite on its own app store and Capcom confirms development of "Resident Evil 9."

headline: Tesla And Reddit Were Among 12 Large Cap Stocks With Strongest Performance In The Last Week (June 30-July 6): Details
summary: Top performing stocks last week: SIRI, TSLA, PARAA, RDDT, LI, IOT, KB, SHG, MDB, SCCO, ZS, NIO. Did you have them in your portfolio?

headline: Steve Ballmer Is Now Worth Just As Much As — If Not More Than — His Old Boss Bill Gates
summary: Former Microsoft CEO Steve Ballmer briefly outpaced the company's co-founder, Bill Gates, in terms of wealth last week, as per Bloomberg's Billionaires Index.

headline: EXCLUSIVE: GLP-1 Weight-Loss Drugs Could Become A Trillion-Dollar Market, Predicts Tema ETFs CEO
summary: The GLP-1 drug market for obesity treatment is expected to skyrocket to $1 trillion or more, according to Maurits Pot, founder and CEO of Tema ETFs.

headline: Nvidia's Dominance, AI Investments, And A Predicted Market Crash: This Week In Artificial Intelligence
summary: Nvidia's Impressive Performance, Elon Musk's Massive Investment in AI, and more. Vistra Corp. tops list of best-performing S&P 500 stocks.

headline: Apple's AI Shift, Board Observer Role At OpenAI, Extended iPhone Settlement Deadline, And AI Partnership With Google: This Week In Appleverse
summary: Apple making moves: focus on AI and software, observer role at OpenAI, iPhone settlement extension, new Vision Pro rumors, and potential AI partnership with Google.
